reference.response:
## Header
- **Program name:** Sarah’s 3-Day Full-Body Strength + Muscle (Body Recomp) Program  
- **Program Owner:** GymText  
- **User:** Sarah Chen  
- **Goal:** Build strength and muscle while improving body composition (fat loss with lean-mass retention/gain)  
- **Duration:** **Ongoing with phase cycling** (repeatable blocks)

---

## Program Philosophy
Sarah is a novice lifter training **3x/week**, so the fastest, safest progress will come from **frequent practice of the basics** (squat pattern, hinge pattern, push, pull, carry, core) with **simple progression**. This program uses a **full-body split** each day to build skill and strength efficiently while keeping weekly volume high enough for hypertrophy and body recomposition. Exercises prioritize stable, gym-friendly options (machines + dumbbells + a few barbell patterns as tolerated) to reduce technique bottlenecks early on. Progression is primarily **double progression + RPE** (adding reps first, then load), with planned deloads to keep recovery and adherence strong.

---

# Phase 1: Foundation & Skill (Weeks 1–4)

### Weekly Pattern (3 sessions/week, e.g., Mon/Wed/Fri)
> **Intensity target:** Mostly **RPE 6–7** (leave ~3–4 reps in reserve early), focus on consistent technique and repeatability.  
> **Rest:** Compounds 90–150s, accessories 45–90s.

## Day 1 — Full Body A (Squat emphasis)
- **Focus:** Squat pattern + horizontal push/pull + core
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) **Leg Press**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Stable squat-pattern strength and quad stimulus without high technical demand early on.*

2) **Dumbbell Bench Press**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
3) **Chest-Supported Row (machine or bench)**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Balances pressing volume and builds upper-back strength for shoulder health and posture.*

4) **Romanian Deadlift (DB or barbell)**: 2 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Posterior-chain strength for body comp and athletic base.*

5) **Cable Crunch**: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**  
6) **Optional finisher:** Incline treadmill walk or bike 8–12 min @ easy-moderate (can talk in sentences)

---

## Day 2 — Full Body B (Hinge emphasis)
- **Focus:** Hinge pattern + vertical push/pull + glutes
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) **Trap Bar Deadlift** (or **DB RDL** if preferred): 3 × 5–8 @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Beginner-friendly hinge that builds full-body strength with manageable technique demands.*

2) **Lat Pulldown**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
3) **Seated DB Shoulder Press** (or machine press): 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
4) **Hip Thrust (machine or barbell)**: 2–3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**  
   *Direct glute work supports lower-body development and body composition goals.*

5) **Leg Curl (machine)**: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**  
6) **Side Plank**: 2 × 20–40 sec/side (stop 1–2 reps short of form breakdown)

---

## Day 3 — Full Body C (Single-leg + hypertrophy balance)
- **Focus:** Single-leg stability + extra upper volume + arms/shoulders
- **Volume:** ~15–20 working sets

1) **Goblet Squat**: 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Grooves squat mechanics, builds legs without needing heavy loading yet.*

2) **Machine Chest Press** (or push-ups): 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**  
3) **Cable Row (neutral grip)**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**  
4) **DB Walking Lunge** (or split squat): 2 × 8–12/leg @ **RPE 7**  
   *Single-leg strength and hip stability for balanced leg development.*

5) **Lateral Raise**: 2 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7–8**  
6) **Triceps Pressdown**: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7–8**  
7) **DB Curl**: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7–8**

---

### Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
- **Main rule (double progression):** Stay within the rep range until you can hit the **top end of reps on all sets** at the target RPE, then add load next time.
  - Example: DB Bench 3×8–12  
    - Week to week aim: 8/8/8 → 9/9/9 → 10/10/10 … until 12/12/12, then add 2–5 lb per dumbbell and return to ~8 reps.
- **RPE guardrails:**  
  - If a set exceeds the target (feels like RPE 8–9), keep weight the same next week (or reduce slightly) and prioritize form.
- **Deload (Week 4):**  
  - Reduce **sets by ~40–50%** (e.g., 3 sets → 1–2 sets) and keep loads similar or slightly lighter, staying **RPE 6**.
- **Transition to Phase 2:**  
  - Move on after Week 4 if technique feels consistent and you’re progressing in reps/loads without excessive soreness or missed reps.

---

# Phase 2: Linear Build (Weeks 5–8)

### Weekly Pattern
Same **3-day full-body** structure, but slightly heavier on the first movement each day.

> **Intensity target:** Main lifts **RPE 7–8**, accessories **RPE 7–8**.  
> **Volume:** Similar weekly sets, but more “work sets” are challenging.

## Day 1 — Full Body A (Squat strength + upper)
- **Leg Press**: 4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **DB Bench Press**: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Chest-Supported Row**: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **RDL (DB or barbell)**: 2–3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Cable Crunch**: 2–3 × 10–15 @ RPE 7–8

## Day 2 — Full Body B (Hinge strength + vertical)
- **Trap Bar Deadlift**: 4 × 4–6 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Lat Pulldown**: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **DB/Machine Shoulder Press**: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Hip Thrust**: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Leg Curl**: 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8  
- **Side Plank**: 2 × 25–45 sec/side

## Day 3 — Full Body C (Hypertrophy + single-leg)
- **Goblet Squat** (or **Hack Squat machine** if available): 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Machine Chest Press**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Cable Row**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Lunge/Split Squat**: 2–3 × 8–12/leg @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Lateral Raise**: 2 × 12–20 @ RPE 8  
- **Triceps Pressdown**: 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8  
- **DB Curl**: 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8

---

### Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Main lifts (first exercise each day):**  
  - Progress load **1–5%** when you complete all sets at the top of the rep range (or when RPE drops below 7 at the same reps).
- **Accessories:** keep double progression; add reps first, then small load increases.
- **Deload (Week 8):** same approach as Phase 1 deload (40–60% volume reduction, RPE ~6).
- **Transition trigger:**  
  - If progress slows (reps stall for 2 straight exposures) or fatigue is accumulating, move to Phase 3.

---

# Phase 3: Resensitization + Variety (Weeks 9–12)

### Weekly Pattern
Same 3 days/week, but slightly lighter and with small variations to keep joints fresh and re-sensitize progress.

> **Intensity target:** Mostly **RPE 6–7** with clean reps; slightly higher reps on accessories.

**Key swaps (choose based on what feels best):**
- Leg Press → **Hack Squat** or **Smith Squat to box**
- Trap Bar Deadlift → **RDL** or **Back Extension**
- DB Bench → **Incline DB Bench** or **Machine Press**
- Rows/Pulldowns stay similar, focus on quality and range

Keep overall set counts similar to Phase 1, with rep ranges biased to **8–15**.

---

### Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- Aim to add **1–2 reps per set** week-to-week until you hit the top of the range, then add a small load increase.
- Deload is optional at the end of Week 12 if recovery is good; otherwise take a deload week before restarting Phase 1 or Phase 2.

---

## Phase Cycling
- **Default cycle:** **Foundation (4w) → Build (4w) → Resensitize (4w) → repeat**
- **What triggers transitions:**
  - **Time-based:** move phases as written every 4 weeks
  - **Performance-based:** if lifts are progressing smoothly and soreness is manageable, you can extend Phase 2 by 2–4 weeks before resensitizing
  - **Recovery-based:** if sleep/stress is high and loads feel unusually heavy for 2+ sessions, deload early and restart the next phase
- **Body composition support (minimal-addition approach):**
  - Optional: 2×/week easy cardio 10–20 minutes after lifting or on off days (kept easy so it doesn’t compromise strength progress)

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created for Sarah Chen (3x/week full-body strength + hypertrophy with phase cycling).

--- 

If you tell me (1) which 3 days you prefer to train, (2) your approximate session length (45/60/75 min), and (3) any exercises you already like/dislike, I can tighten exercise selection further and set a more precise weekly time budget.
